name: Sea Bass Veracruzana
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Sea bass, skinned and boned
Or other firm fleshed fresh fish, cut into 4 portions
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 small Yellow onion, peeled and thinly sliced
2 small Clove garlic, peeled and minced
1 1/2 Jalapenos, stemmed and sliced into 1/4 inch disks (2 to 3)
3/4 Tomato, cored and seeded, cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat one very large or two medium-sized saute pans over medium-high heat for a minute, then add butter or oil and melt.
2. When sizzling, add fish filets seasoned with salt and pepper, flesh side down.
3. Reduce the heat to high and sear the filets until golden brown. Flip and sear on the other side.
4. Remove the filets from the pan and set them aside on a rack over a plate to catch juices. Return the pan to the heat.
5. Add onions and cook, stirring often over high heat for 2-3 minutes.
6. Add garlic, jalapeno slices, lime wedges, tomatoes, oregano, and olives, and sauté briskly for an additional 30 seconds.
7. Pour in white wine and reduce to half.
8. Add fish stock or clam juice and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to a simmer.
9. Return the fish filets along with the juices to the pan and finish cooking, covered, about 1-3 minutes depending on the thickness of the filets.
10. Taste the broth and adjust seasoning (salt, pepper, oregano) as needed before serving immediately in soup plates with a generous puddle of broth and garnish of vegetables atop.
